1: Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
An Israelite. prophet. The same as yownah; Jonah, An Israelite.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
True, the father of Jonah. From 'emeth; veracious; Amittai, an Israelite.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
Capital of Assyr. Of foreign origin; Nineveh, the capital of Assyria.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
An Israelite. prophet. The same as yownah; Jonah, An Israelite.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
To go through, flee. A primitive root; to bolt, i.e. Figuratively, to flee suddenly.
A son of Javan, his desc. and their land, also a port on the Mediterranean, also a Benjamite, also a Pers. noble.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To come or go down, descend.
A seaport city of Pal. Or Yaphow; from yaphah; beautiful; Japho, a place in Palestine.
A ship. Feminine of 'oniy; a ship.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
A son of Javan, his desc. and their land, also a port on the Mediterranean, also a Benjamite, also a Pers. noble.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Hire, wages. From sakar; payment of contract; concretely, salary, fare, maintenance; by implication, compensation, benefit.
To come or go down, descend.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.

A ship. Feminine of 'oniy; a ship.
To break, break in pieces. A primitive root; to burst.
A mariner. From malach in its second. Sense; a sailor.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To cry, cry out, call. A primitive root; to shriek; by analogy, to announce or convene publicly.

To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
and fell into a deep sleep.
To be in or fall into heavy sleep. A primitive root; to stun, i.e. Stupefy.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Sailor. Active participle from chabal; a sailor.
To come near, approach. A primitive root; to approach for whatever purpose.

To be sleek, glossy, to excogitate. A primitive root; probably to be sleek, i.e. Glossy; hence to excogitate.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.

Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Where?. Perhaps from 'aiyn; where? Hence how?.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.

1: A fish. Feminine of dag, and meaning the same.
An Israelite. prophet. The same as yownah; Jonah, An Israelite.
To intervene, interpose. A primitive root; to judge; by extension, to intercede, pray.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
2: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
From an unused root probably meaning to be hollow; the belly, especially the womb; also the bosom or body of anything.
Or shol; from sha'al; Hades or the world of the dead, including its accessories and inmates.
To cry out (for help). A primitive root; properly, to be free; but used only causatively and reflexively, to halloo.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
3: To throw, fling, cast. A primitive root; to throw out, down or away.
Depth, deep. Or mtsolah; also mtsuwlah; or mtsulah; from the same as tsuwlah; a deep place.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
A primitive root; to revolve, surround, or border; used in various applications, literally and figuratively.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A breaker (of the sea). From shabar; a breaker.
A heap, wave, billow. From galal; something rolled, i.e. A heap of stone or dung, by analogy, a spring of water.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
4: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To drive out, cast out. A primitive root; to drive out from a possession; especially to expatriate or divorce.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
To look. A primitive root; to scan, i.e. Look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
A palace, temple. Probably from yakol; a large public building, such as a palace or temple.
5: Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To surround, encompass. A primitive root; to surround.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Deep, sea, abyss. Or thom; from huwm; an abyss, especially the deep.
A primitive root; to revolve, surround, or border; used in various applications, literally and figuratively.
Reeds, rushes. Probably of Egyptian origin; a reed, especially the papyrus.
To bind, bind on, bind up. A primitive root; to wrap firmly; figuratively, to stop, to rule.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
6: A cut, shape, extremity. From qatsab; shape; base.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
To come or go down, descend.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
From ad with prepositional prefix; in up to or over against; generally at, beside, among, behind, for, etc.
A bar. From barach; a bolt.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
A pit. From shuwach; a pit; figuratively, destruction.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God

Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.

Prayer. From palal; intercession, supplication; by implication, a hymn.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
A palace, temple. Probably from yakol; a large public building, such as a palace or temple.
8: To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
Or shav; from the same as show' in the sense of desolating; evil, literally or morally; figuratively idolatry, uselessness.
Or Habel; from habal; emptiness or vanity; figuratively, something transitory and unsatisfactory; often used as an adverb.
To loosen, relinquish, permit. A primitive root; to loosen, i.e. Relinquish, permit, etc.
Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.
9: Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
From yadah; properly, an extension of the hand, i.e. avowal, or adoration; specifically, a choir of worshippers.
To slaughter for sacrifice. A primitive root; to slaughter an animal.
A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To vow. A primitive root; to promise.
Salvation. Feminine passive participle of yasha'; something saved, i.e. deliverance; hence, aid, victory, prosperity.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.

Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
A three, triad. Or shalosh; masculine shlowshah; or shloshah; a primitive number; three; occasionally third, or Thrice.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
A walk, journey, a going. From halak; a walk, i.e. A passage or a distance.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
A walk, journey, a going. From halak; a walk, i.e. A passage or a distance.
An Israelite. prophet. The same as yownah; Jonah, An Israelite.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
A primitive root; properly, to bore, i.e. to wound, to dissolve; figuratively, to profane, to break, to begin; denom. to play.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Capital of Assyr. Of foreign origin; Nineveh, the capital of Assyria.
To turn, overturn. A primitive root; to turn about or over; by implication, to change, overturn, return, pervert.
5: Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
Capital of Assyr. Of foreign origin; Nineveh, the capital of Assyria.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Fasting, a fast. Or tsom; from from tsuwm; a fast.
Or labesh; a primitive root; properly, wrap around, i.e. to put on a garment or clothe, literally or figuratively.
Sack, sackcloth. From shaqaq; properly, a mesh, i.e. Coarse loose cloth or sacking; hence, a bag.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Small, young, unimportant. Or qaton; from quwt; abbreviated, i.e. Diminutive, literally or figuratively.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
Capital of Assyr. Of foreign origin; Nineveh, the capital of Assyria.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
Seat of honor, throne. Or kicceh; from kacah; properly, covered, i.e. A throne.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
Glory, a cloak. Feminine of 'addiyr; something ample; also the same as 'eder.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
Sack, sackcloth. From shaqaq; properly, a mesh, i.e. Coarse loose cloth or sacking; hence, a bag.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Ashes. From an unused root meaning to bestrew; ashes.
To cry, cry out, call. A primitive root; to shriek; by analogy, to announce or convene publicly.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Capital of Assyr. Of foreign origin; Nineveh, the capital of Assyria.
Taste, judgment. From ta'am; properly, a taste, i.e. perception; by implication, intelligence; transitively, a mandate.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
A beast, animal, cattle. From an unused root; properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal.
Cattle, herd, an ox. From baqar; beef cattle or an animal of the ox family of either gender; collectively, a herd.
Small cattle, sheep and goats, flock. Or tsaown; from an unused root meaning to migrate; a collective name for a flock; also figuratively.
To taste, perceive. A primitive root; to taste; figuratively, to perceive.
Anything. Apparently a form of m'uwm; properly, a speck or point, i.e. something; with negative, nothing.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
A primitive root; to tend a flock; i.e. Pasture it; intransitively, to graze; generally to rule; by extension, to associate with.
To drink. A primitive root; to imbibe.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
A beast, animal, cattle. From an unused root; properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
Sack, sackcloth. From shaqaq; properly, a mesh, i.e. Coarse loose cloth or sacking; hence, a bag.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Strength, force, violence. Feminine of chozeq; vehemence.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
A primitive root; properly, to sigh, i.e. Breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. to pity, console or rue; or to avenge.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
(burning of) anger. Or charon; from charah; a burning of anger.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
10: Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
A deed, work. From asah; an action; generally, a transaction; abstractly, activity; by implication, a product or property.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.

A primitive root; properly, to sigh, i.e. Breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. to pity, console or rue; or to avenge.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.

1: An Israelite. prophet. The same as yownah; Jonah, An Israelite.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
A primitive root; properly, to spoil; figuratively, to make good for nothing, i.e. Bad.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
To burn or be kindled with anger. A primitive root; to glow or grow warm; figuratively to blaze up, of anger, zeal, jealousy.
To intervene, interpose. A primitive root; to judge; by extension, to intercede, pray.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Ah, now! I (we) beseech you! Or mannah; apparent contracted from 'ahabah and na'; oh now!
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
To come or be in front, meet. A primitive root; to project, i.e. Precede; hence, to anticipate, hasten, meet.
To go through, flee. A primitive root; to bolt, i.e. Figuratively, to flee suddenly.
A son of Javan, his desc. and their land, also a port on the Mediterranean, also a Benjamite, also a Pers. noble.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.
A primitive root; properly, to sigh, i.e. Breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. to pity, console or rue; or to avenge.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Now. From eth; at this time, whether adverb, conjunction or expletive.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Death. From muwth; death; concretely, the dead, their place or state; figuratively, pestilence, ruin.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To be good, well, glad, or pleasing. A primitive root; to be make well, literally or figuratively.
To burn or be kindled with anger. A primitive root; to glow or grow warm; figuratively to blaze up, of anger, zeal, jealousy.

Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.

A thicket, booth. Fem of cok; a hut or lair.

Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
A shadow. From tsalal; shade, whether literal or figurative.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.

Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.

Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
Rejoice, be glad. A primitive root; probably to brighten up, i.e. be blithe or gleesome.

From an unused root meaning to be brilliant; the sun; by implication, the east; figuratively, a ray, i.e. A notched battlement.
To rise, come forth. A primitive root; properly, to irradiate, i.e. To rise; specifically, to appear.

Perhaps sultry. From charash in the sense of silence; quiet, i.e. Sultry.
East, east wind. Or qadim; from qadam; the fore or front part; hence the East.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.

To ask, inquire. Or shael; a primitive root; to inquire; by implication, to request; by extension, to demand.

To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.

To labor, toil. A primitive root; to toil, i.e. Work severely and with irksomeness.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To grow up, become great. A primitive root; properly, to twist, i.e. To be large.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.

Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To be or become much, many or great. A primitive root; to increase.
Two (a card. number). Dual of sheniy; feminine shttayim; two; also twofold.
Ten. For eser; ten, i.e. -teen; also -teenth.
Ten thousand, myriad. From rabab; or ribbow from rabab; a myriad, i.e. Indefinitely, large number.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
A beast, animal, cattle. From an unused root; properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal.
